description_long: This irregularly shaped stone features a rugged, natural surface with a mix of rounded and angular edges. Its coloration is predominantly gray with subtle brown and darker mottled patches, suggesting exposure to the elements. The stone has several shallow depressions and pitted areas, giving it a weathered appearance. Its surface texture is coarse and uneven, with visible grain and minor cracks. The overall form is asymmetrical, tapering to a thinner edge on one side and bulging outward on the opposite side. This stone would be commonly found as a loose obstacle or decorative element in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas.
description: A rugged, asymmetrical gray stone with coarse texture, pitted depressions, and mottled surface coloration. It appears weathered and naturally shaped, suitable for urban outdoor settings.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's broad, uneven face with several shallow depressions and a prominent bulge on the upper right.
description_view_1: The stone appears elongated and tapers to a thinner edge, with subtle dark mottling visible on the upper surface.
description_view_2: A flatter side is visible, showing a smoother surface with minor cracks and a gentle curve along the bottom edge.
description_view_3: This angle reveals the stone's thickest section, with a rough, grainy texture and a pronounced vertical rise.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loose obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and pitting with no regular pattern; subtle color variations and natural grain visible.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with minor cracks, indicating moderate weathering and prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones; may contrast with paved surfaces but integrates well in landscaped areas.
